U
Oracle Events
OE1: Look up Tyler Okonkwo's relocation record using airtable_search_records (or airtable_list_records) (field_name: "Name", value: "Tyler Okonkwo" or similar). Expected discoveries: origin Philadelphia, PA; destination Denver, CO; Denver address 2150 Lawrence St, Apt 4B, Denver CO 80205; no hotel needed (employee staying with family during gap); special items — carbon-frame road bike (~$3,500, requires professional bike boxing) and 65" TV (standard TV boxing); total storage volume ~450 cu ft (2-bedroom); dates shifted +1 day from original April 11–13 to April 12–14; temp storage required (~13-day gap until May 1 lease start); coordinator Javier Morales; AM Catalina Dubois.
OE2: Update Tyler Okonkwo's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records to reflect the one-day date shift confirmed by the client (Move Start Date: 2026-04-12, Move End Date: 2026-04-14, or update Special Requirements to note the confirmed shift).
OE3: Send an email to Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations requesting a quote for Tyler Okonkwo's temporary storage using send_email (sender: "javier.morales@moveops.com", recipients: "greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com", subject/content: quote for temporary storage of ~450 cu ft household goods in Denver, or similar).
OE4: Look up Marcus Webb's relocation record using airtable_search_records (or airtable_list_records) (field_name: "Name", value: "Marcus Webb" or similar) to check the current status of his Detroit-to-Chicago relocation (April 10–12) and whether the original schedule overlap with Tyler's move has been fully resolved.
OE5: Search for an existing Linear issue tracking the schedule conflict between Tyler Okonkwo's and Marcus Webb's moves using linear_list_issues (query: "schedule" or "conflict" or "Marcus" or "overlap" or similar). Expected: no existing tracking issue found.
OE6: Edit this: This is untrue. No linear issue needs to be created since the email correspondence makes it seem like the overlap was fixed with the 1-day shift. Thus, it's better to err on this side of not creating the issue
~~Create a new high-priority Linear issue to track the scheduling conflict using linear_create_issue (team: "Operations" or similar, title: "Schedule conflict: Tyler Okonkwo / Marcus Webb move overlap" or similar, priority: 1 or similar).~~
OE7: Search contacts to check whether Tyler Okonkwo's work email has already been added using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Tyler Okonkwo" or "Tyler" or similar). Expected: no result found — Tyler Okonkwo does not exist in Javier's contacts.
OE8: Create a contact for Tyler Okonkwo using contacts_add_new_contact (first_name: "Tyler", last_name: "Okonkwo", email: "tyler.okonkwo@canopyhealth.io" or similar work email).
OE9: Search the calendar for events on the move days using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-12 00:00:00", end_datetime: "2026-04-14 23:59:59"). Expected: calendar is clear (no events found).
OE10: Add calendar time blocks on each move day so Javier can check in with the client using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Tyler Okonkwo move check-in" or similar, start_datetime/end_datetime covering April 12 and April 13 or 14).
OE11: Send an email from Javier to Chloe with a cc to Catalina summarizing the full relocation progress using send_email (sender: "javier.morales@moveops.com", recipients: "chloe.vance@moveops.com", cc: "catalina.dubois@moveops.com", content: progress update covering Tyler Okonkwo's relocation status, or similar).
